response:
TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED BY ADDING THE AREAS OF EACH CIRCLE. THE RADIUS OF EACH CIRCLE INCREASES BY 10% FROM THE PREVIOUS ONE. THE FORMULA FOR THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR². FOR THE FIRST CIRCLE, THE AREA IS πR². FOR THE SECOND CIRCLE, THE RADIUS IS 1.1R, SO THE AREA IS π(1.1R)². THIS PATTERN CONTINUES UNTIL THE EIGHTH CIRCLE. THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF ALL THESE AREAS. THE RESULT IS EXPRESSED AS A FUNCTION OF R AND π. THE SUM CAN BE WRITTEN AS πR²(1 + (1.1)² + (1.1)⁴ + ... + (1.1)¹⁴). THIS SERIES REPRESENTS THE GROWING RADIUS SQUARED FOR EACH CIRCLE. THE SUM OF THE SERIES IS THEN MULTIPLIED BY πR² TO OBTAIN THE FINAL TOTAL AREA. THIS METHOD ENSURES ACCURACY AND CLARITY IN CALCULATING THE TOTAL AREA. THE EXPRESSION REMAINS IN TERMS OF R AND π AS REQUIRED.

THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE FIFTH CIRCLE IS USED TO PLACE TEXT ALONG ITS EDGE. THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS 2πR, WHERE R IS THE RADIUS OF THE FIFTH CIRCLE. THE RADIUS INCREASES BY 10% FOR EACH CIRCLE, SO THE FIFTH CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS OF (1.1)⁴R. THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS THEN 2π(1.1)⁴R. EACH CHARACTER AND SPACE OCCUPIES A TOTAL OF 0.5 CM. TO DETERMINE THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS, DIVIDE THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY 0.5. THIS GIVES THE TOTAL NUMBER OF CHARACTERS AND SPACES. THE RESULT MUST BE AN INTEGER, SO THE MAXIMUM NUMBER IS THE INTEGER PART OF THE DIVISION. THIS ENSURES THE TEXT IS EVENLY SPACED AND FITS PERFECTLY ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER IS CALCULATED BY DIVIDING THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY 0.5 AND TAKING THE FLOOR OF THE RESULT. THIS METHOD MEETS THE DESIGNER
